examples.title: Singly Linked List Implementation
examples.description: A complete implementation of a singly linked list with Node class, insertion at head/tail, deletion, and traversal operations.
examples.explanation: The singly linked list stores nodes with data and a pointer to the next node. The Node class holds the data and reference. The SinglyLinkedList class manages operations: append adds to end, prepend adds to beginning, delete removes by value, and display prints all elements. Each node only links forward, hence 'singly'.

examples.title: Circular Doubly Linked List
examples.description: Implementation of a circular doubly linked list where each node has both previous and next pointers, and the last node points back to head.
examples.explanation: A circular doubly linked list combines bidirectional links with circular connectivity. Each node has prev and next pointers, allowing traversal in both directions. The last node's next points to head, and head's prev points to tail, creating a circle. This implementation handles edge cases like single node deletion and maintains circular references throughout all operations.
